title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While investigating a cave, Lane retrieves a deep green iron box labeled "sandwich" from a high rock crevice. Inspector Philip examines the contents, finding small containers for salt, pepper, and mustard. While the salt and pepper containers are filled with salt, a rectangular flat box contains a bitter, white crystalline powder that Philip identifies as a drug.

At the hotel, the police commissioner, Colonel Weston, and Hercule Poirot discuss the implications of the drug. Weston suggests the victim, Mrs. Marshall, may have been killed for stumbling upon a drug cartel, while Poirot doubts she was an addict due to her healthy appearance. Horace Blatt, a local man, arrives at the hotel and joins the discussion. Blatt claims he was sailing alone toward Plymouth at 9:45 a.m. and suggests the murder was a crime of passion. He provides character details about Marshall, noting he is a "dark horse" who has previously been involved in physical altercations.

When questioned about whom Mrs. Marshall might have been visiting at Fairy Bay, Blatt suggests Redfin, Gardner, Barry, or a clergyman, whom he describes as a hypocrite. Blatt also expresses his dissatisfaction with the unfriendly atmosphere at the local establishment, the Pirate's Flag Inn. Poirot observes that Blatt appears "tense." Meanwhile, Inspector Cargraves notes that it takes three minutes to walk from the hotel to the elevator leading to Fairy Bay, suggesting a person could reach the area quickly once out of sight of other guests.
